2603.17558.title: Zipper-LoRA: Dynamic Parameter Decoupling for Speech-LLM based Multilingual Speech Recognition
2603.17558.paper_summary: Speech Large Language Models (Speech-LLMs) have emerged as a powerful approach for automatic speech recognition (ASR) by aligning speech encoders with large language models. However, adapting these systems to multilingual settings with imbalanced data distributions remains challenging. In such scenarios, a stability-plasticity dilemma often arises: fully shared Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) can cause negative inter-lingual interference for under-represented languages, while fully language-specific tuning limits the cross-lingual beneficial knowledge transfer needed for low-resource tasks. To address this, we propose Zipper-LoRA, a novel rank-level decoupling framework with three variants (Static, Hard, and Soft) that dynamically synthesizes LoRA updates from shared and language-specific subspaces. By using a lightweight language-conditioned router, Zipper-LoRA dynamically controls the contribution of each subspace at the LoRA rank level, enabling fine-grained sharing where languages are compatible and strict decoupling when conflicts occur. To further stabilize optimization under imbalanced data, we propose a two-stage training strategy with an Initial-B warm start that significantly accelerates convergence. Experiments on a 12-language mixed-resource setting show that Zipper-LoRA consistently outperforms both fully shared and independent baselines, particularly in extremely low-resource scenarios. Moreover, we demonstrate that these gains are robust across both chunked and non-chunked encoder configurations, confirming the framework's reliability for practical, large-scale multilingual ASR. Our code and data will be available at https://github.com/YuCeong-May/Zipper-LoRA for reproducibility.
2603.17558.paper_summary_zh: 語音大型語言模型（Speech-LLMs）已成為自動語音識別（ASR）的強大方法，通過將語音編碼器與大型語言模型對齊。然而，將這些系統適應於數據分佈不平衡的多語言環境仍然具有挑戰性。在這種情況下，經常會出現穩定性-可塑性困境：完全共享的參數高效微調（PEFT）可能會對代表性不足的語言造成負面的跨語言干擾，而完全語言特定的調整則限制了低資源任務所需的跨語言有益知識轉移。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了Zipper-LoRA，一種新穎的秩級解耦框架，具有三種變體（靜態、硬性和軟性），能夠動態合成來自共享和語言特定子空間的LoRA更新。通過使用輕量級的語言條件路由器，Zipper-LoRA在LoRA秩級別上動態控制每個子空間的貢獻，實現語言兼容時的細粒度共享，以及在衝突發生時的嚴格解耦。為了進一步穩定不平衡數據下的優化，我們提出了一種兩階段的訓練策略，具有初始-B熱啟動，顯著加速收斂。在12種語言的混合資源設置下的實驗顯示，Zipper-LoRA在極低資源場景中特別超越了完全共享和獨立基準。此外，我們證明這些增益在分塊和非分塊編碼器配置中都是穩健的，確認了該框架在實際大型多語言ASR中的可靠性。我們的代碼和數據將在https://github.com/YuCeong-May/Zipper-LoRA上提供，以便重現。
2603.17558.author: Yuxiang Mei et.al.
2603.17558.authors: Yuxiang Mei, Delai Qiu, Shengping Liu, Jiaen Liang, Yanhua Long
